title.en: SMALLARMS, e.g. PISTOLS, RIFLES (functional features or details common to both smallarms and ordnance, mountings therefor F41A; projecting missiles without use of explosive or combustible propellant charge F41B); ACCESSORIES THEREFOR
Pistols, e.g. revolvers (specially adapted for underwater use F41C9/06; for slaughtering or stunning animals A22B; for shooting bolts into concrete constructions, metal walls or the like B25C)
{Toy guns, e.g. cannons, firing a projectile by means of the combustion of a propellant charge, e.g. a cap}
Signal pistols, e.g. Very pistols
Starting pistols; Alarm pistols {(F41C3/06 takes precedence)}
Cap-firing pistols, e.g. toy pistols

Revolvers (F41C3/10 takes precedence; sealing aspects F41A3/76; using a revolving drum magazine for feeding revolver-type guns other than revolvers F41A9/27; extractors or ejectors therefor F41A15/02)
Shoulder-fired smallarms, e.g. rifles, carbines, shotguns (specially adapted for underwater use F41C9/06)
Pump-action guns, i.e. guns having a reciprocating handgrip beneath the barrel for loading or cocking
with reciprocating handgrip under the buttstock for loading or cocking
Lever-action guns, i.e. guns having a rocking lever for loading or cocking
Breakdown shotguns or rifles (hinge-frame revolvers F41C3/16; breech mechanisms therefor F41A3/58; cocking mechanisms therefor F41A19/41)
{Gallery rifles; Parlour rifles}
Other smallarms, e.g. hidden smallarms or smallarms specially adapted for underwater use
Concealed pistols, e.g. in pencils
Walking-stick guns {(sticks combined with weapons A45B3/14)}
Smallarms specially adapted for underwater use {(spring operated harpoon guns F41B7/04; air pressure operated harpoon guns F41B11/83)}
Muzzle-loading smallarms; Smallarms with flintlock mechanisms; Accessories therefor
Butts; Butt plates; Stocks {(handles for carrying smallarms F41C33/08)}
Attachment of slings
Folding or telescopic stocks or stock parts (articulated or collapsible guns F41A11/04)
Stocks {or firearm frames} specially adapted for recoil reduction
Stocks or grips for pistols, e.g. revolvers (F41C23/12 takes precedence)
Auxiliary stocks for stabilising, or for transforming pistols, e.g. revolvers, into shoulder-fired guns
Adjustable stock or stock parts, i.e. adaptable to personal requirements, e.g. length, pitch, cast or drop
Forestocks; Handgrips; Hand guards (pump-action guns F41C7/02)
characterised by the material used (F41C23/08 takes precedence)
Butts; Butt plates; Mountings therefor (F41C23/08, F41C23/10, {F41C23/14} take precedence)
Stocks having space for the storage of objects
Accessories; Details or attachments not otherwise provided for
Arrangements for mounting spades or shields (spades per se A01B1/02; shields per se F41H5/06)
Adaptations of smallarms for firing grenades, e.g. rifle grenades, or for firing riot-control ammunition; Barrel attachments therefor (signal pistols F41C3/02)
Smallarms combined with thrusting or cutting weapons (thrusting or cutting weapons other than bayonets F41B13/00); Bayonets; Bayonet mounts
Attachments for wire cutting
Balancing or stabilising arrangements {on the gun itself, e.g. balancing weights}
Means for wearing or carrying smallarms
{Shooting harnesses; Stabilising devices, e.g. straps on the body (balancing arrangements on the gun itself F41C27/22)}
{Slings for carrying longguns, e.g. straps (attachment of slings F41C23/02; shooting harnesses F41C33/001)}
{Buttstock supports, i.e. devices for carrying the weight of long firearms in a substantially vertical orientation by supporting the buttstock (holsters F41C33/02)}
{Clips, hooks or the like, for carrying the weight of smallarms (holsters F41C33/02)}
Holsters, i.e. cases for pistols having means for being carried or worn, e.g. at the belt or under the arm
Containers for carrying smallarms, e.g. safety boxes, gun cases (F41C33/02 takes precedence)
Handles for carrying smallarms
